mechanism_narrative: A lower or falling FEDFUNDS level raises the present value of future earnings and reduces cash competition for equities, producing positive S&P 500 20-day forward returns when policy is in an easing stance.
failure_condition: Reject if lower 3-month changes in FEDFUNDS do not produce higher 20-day S&P 500 forward returns than higher changes in at least 60% of rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE above recessionary levels signals deteriorating household income and weaker expected corporate revenues, producing negative S&P 500 20-day forward returns after the labor deterioration is observed.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE crosses above 8.0% or rises by more than 0.5pp over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations have positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of cases or if the mean triggered return is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS rises while UNRATE remains high, consumer spending is recovering before labor slack has normalized, signaling an early-cycle demand rebound that produces positive S&P 500 60-day forward returns.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition does not outperform periods with UNRATE > 8.0% and non-rising RSAFS by at least 1 percentage point over 60 trading days.

mechanism_narrative: A positive turn in INDPRO after depressed production signals restocking and operating leverage in cyclical sectors, producing positive S&P 500 60-day forward returns as earnings expectations improve.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises by more than 1.0% over 3 months after a negative 12-month change
failure_condition: Reject if triggered INDPRO rebounds have a nonpositive average 60-day S&P 500 forward return or fail to beat non-triggered periods in rolling 5-year tests.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in HOUST below deeply depressed levels signals weak construction demand, impaired credit transmission, and poor household formation, producing negative S&P 500 60-day forward returns.
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST-below-600 triggers are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or do not underperform unconditional returns.

mechanism_narrative: FEDFUNDS near the zero lower bound combined with high UNRATE signals that policy support is responding to severe macro stress rather than normal easing, producing negative S&P 500 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint FEDFUNDS and UNRATE trigger produces higher 20-day returns than low-FEDFUNDS periods with UNRATE <= 7.0% across a full walk-forward sample.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in CPIAUCSL while FEDFUNDS remains pinned near zero signals rising inflation pressure without policy normalization, increasing discount-rate uncertainty and producing negative 5-day S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 6-month annualized change exceeds 3.0% and FEDFUNDS < 0.5%
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations do not have a negative average 5-day S&P 500 forward return or if the short-horizon hit rate is below 52% in the predicted negative direction.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS rises after weak GDPC1, payroll improvement signals that firms are rehiring ahead of fully visible output recovery, producing positive S&P 500 60-day forward returns in the recovery phase.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS recovery and weak GDPC1 condition fails to deliver positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in at least 55% of triggered instances.
